questions.q: What is the difference between 'Acceptance' and 'Endorsement' of a bill?
questions.answer: Acceptance is the act of the drawee acknowledging the bill while endorsement is the act of the holder transferring the bill to another person.

questions.q: Who bears the loss in case of an unaccepted bill?
What is the purpose of a 'Notary Stamp' on a bill?
What are the different types of bills of exchange?
questions.answer: The different types of bills of exchange are: Bearer Bill, Order Bill, Acceptance Bill and Draft.

questions.q: What is the difference between 'Bearer Bill' and 'Order Bill'?
questions.answer: Bearer Bill: It is a bill of exchange which is payable to the bearer. Order Bill: It is a bill of exchange which is payable to the order of the payee.

questions.q: What is the process of drawing a bill?
questions.answer: The process of drawing a bill involves writing the bill on a non-judicial paper in a formal way including the details of the bill.

questions.q: What is the function of a 'Bill of Exchange'?
What is the difference between 'Bill of Exchange' and 'Promissory Note'?
questions.answer: Bill of Exchange: It is a written order by a person to his banker or agent to pay a certain sum to another person. Promissory Note: It is a written agreement by a person to pay a certain sum to another person.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a 'Draft'?
questions.answer: to send funds to a different place

questions.q: What are the different stages in the life cycle of a bill?
questions.answer: The different stages in the life cycle of a bill are: Creation, Negotiation, Dishonour and Protest.
